rag_rules: Preserve the AADL architecture by inserting AGREE annex blocks only in valid component type or implementation scopes
Use component type annexes for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equation declarations
Place implementation-level bindings in component implementation annexes
Avoid using subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
generic_rules: Insert AGREE annex blocks in the appropriate scope to maintain architectural integrity
Use visible identifiers that are already in scope to ensure valid references
Preserve the original AADL structure and relationships during fusion
Avoid assumptions or guarantees that reference subcomponent ports in component type contracts
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Use exact Boolean expressions only when the port is an AGREE bool in scope
Avoid encoding AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions unless explicitly exposed as ports or constants
anti_patterns: Do not insert AGREE annex blocks in invalid scopes that disrupt the AADL architecture
Avoid assumptions or guarantees that reference subcomponent ports in component type contracts
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions unless explicitly exposed as ports or constants
irrelevant_topics: AGREE, annex, assume, guarantee, assign, validator, or repair
